proofs.scope: Known-user failure, unknown-user failure, identical non-enumerating copy, and successful password login against the disposable lab.
Proves admin module and attention entries are absent for the no-grants requester; direct-route and API authorization remain separate proofs.

Requester proxy authorization resolves metadata without decrypting secrets and binds every read to the same deterministic effective Radarr/Sonarr instance or exact Chaptarr grant exposed in config. Only complete service- and version-bound read routes pass, including the narrow owned-book MediaCover path and exact Chaptarr book lookup; hidden siblings, broader subroutes, writes, commands, release search, config, non-arr or unknown targets, traversal, missing instances, and unavailable lookups fail closed. Admins bypass requester classification.
Interactive provider and MCP tool dispatch rechecks current role, device, and shared grant state and fails closed after revocation.
Pull-request CI runs go vet over every server package and fails on any diagnostic.
Pull-request CI runs the complete Go test suite once without retry wrappers and fails when any package fails.
Pull-request CI produces the server command with CGO disabled and fails if the build does not complete.
Pull-request CI runs the project's required Flutter analyzer command and enforces its exit status.
Pull-request CI runs the complete Flutter unit and widget suite and fails when any test fails.
CI proves the release web build succeeds; serving the output and checking the initial route and browser console/runtime remain unproved.
Docker CI builds the root image and inspects its bundled Codex runtime; starting Cantinarr with an empty volume and checking health and the web app remain unproved.

evidence.selector: Build published image for smoke test (BASE-007 partial)
Verify published-image Codex runtime and notices (BASE-007/BASE-009 partial)

proofs.scope: Docker CI builds the server-only image and inspects its bundled Codex runtime; starting Cantinarr and checking its healthy API state remain unproved.

evidence.selector: Build server-only image for smoke test (BASE-008 partial)
Verify server-only Codex runtime and notices (BASE-008/BASE-009 partial)

proofs.scope: Docker CI verifies the pinned Codex runtime and notices in the root image on amd64 and optionally arm64, and in the server-only image on amd64; server-only arm64 remains unproved on ordinary runs.

evidence.selector: Verify published-image Codex runtime and notices (BASE-007/BASE-009 partial)
Verify arm64 Codex runtime and notices (BASE-009 partial)
Verify server-only Codex runtime and notices (BASE-008/BASE-009 partial)

proofs.scope: CI checksum-verifies the pinned app-server and enables the real command-surface smoke test; a model turn and explicit clean-shutdown assertion remain unproved.

evidence.selector: Verify and enable real Codex protocol smoke (BASE-010 partial)

proofs.scope: The release workflow builds a signed IPA; the local swiftc fallback and launch on a physical iOS device remain unproved.
The release workflow builds the signed Android AAB; physical installation, launch, networking, deep links, and passkey registration remain unproved.

Nested and case-varied API keys, authorization/cookie fields, dynamic credential value/header shapes, URL userinfo, WHATWG whitespace/backslash variants, decorated URLs, nested encoded redirect URLs, repeated/encoded secret query and fragment parameters, and strict secret-bearing response URL headers are recursively scrubbed while ordinary data and routing remain intact.
Issue creation deduplicates the same incident scope while retaining same-title incidents on distinct instances.
Proves database repair and handoff recovery preserve an unknown outcome requiring admin review; full startup recovery against the repaired state and no-second-proposal behavior remain unproved.
A partial executor result is retained as an ambiguous outcome requiring admin review and is never reported as a clean failure or resolution.
Autonomous remediation ignores the reporter's personal AI settings and grants, uses only the server-owned shared resolver, and fails closed without credential fallback.
Proves title-level book mutations fail closed and a durable queue identity is accepted; manual-import and mismatched-identity vectors remain unproved.
The complete tool definition matrix is role-classified, requester enumeration excludes admin tools, and direct execution of a known admin tool is denied before dispatch.
Proves the fixed admin sees the complete configured module inventory; route entry, page navigation, breakpoints, back behavior, and preserved stacks remain unproved.
Compares fixed lab admin and requester navigation for configured services; conditional service and attention-state permutations remain unproved.
An explicit push-gateway key suppresses enrollment, authenticates a successful notification send, and is never persisted to the settings table.
Proves request-pending recipient selection remains admin-scoped despite requester opt-in; issue, agent-action, and Plex-access categories remain unproved.

Proves the current list has no Dismissible, uses an explicitly labeled confirmation path, and leaves delete-files unchecked; active swipe, long-press, and other unlabeled-gesture vectors remain unproved.

evidence.selector: menu shows a confirmation with delete-files unchecked

proofs.scope: The repository has a deterministic multi-device screenshot runner; build integration, required-image completeness, fastlane placement, credential review, and clipping review remain unproved.

The matching authenticated encryption canary is accepted; tampered ciphertext or authentication tags, a wrong key, an authenticated wrong value, and a plaintext replacement fail closed and leave the exact stored bytes unchanged. A credential-registry consumer likewise returns no plaintext, logs no credential material, and preserves the exact persisted encrypted value after tampering or a wrong-key read.

Incoming authorization, cookie (including legacy Cookie2), session-token, proxy-authorization, API-key, identity-assertion, forwarding, proxy-chain, client-IP, origin/referrer, routing/method-override, and protocol-upgrade metadata plus request trailers are stripped after hop-by-hop processing. Only the stored upstream API key and identity encoding are injected, the configured upstream host is applied, safe paths/queries/ordinary headers survive, CONNECT is rejected before instance resolution, and an upstream 101 is rejected before opaque tunnel bytes can reach a client.

SSE on the intended exact arr event route is bounded, scrubbed event-by-event, and remains usable and unbuffered. Wrong-route, mislabeled, malformed, mixed-type, or unsafe-first-event SSE and structured JSON streams fail closed without reflecting upstream secrets, while intended opaque text and binary responses remain byte-identical and unbuffered.

Raw requester and admin config responses are constrained to exact safe top-level and per-instance shapes; requesters receive only effective defaults and grants, including a stable unflagged Radarr/Sonarr fallback marked as effective. A defaults/grants lookup failure returns an opaque no-store 503 before any global or hidden-instance listing. Admins receive all instance metadata, and neither successful response can contain instance, credential-registry, webhook, or server-config secret sentinels.
